The HTML fragment 'globals-table.xhtml' is not included anywhere in the produced implementation guide
Published code systems SHOULD conform to the ShareableCodeSystem profile, which says that the element CodeSystem.experimental is mandatory, but it is not present
Published code systems SHOULD conform to the ShareableCodeSystem profile, which says that the element CodeSystem.experimental is mandatory, but it is not present
CodeSystems SHOULD have a stated value for the caseSensitive element so that users know the status and meaning of the code system clearly
Published code systems SHOULD conform to the ShareableCodeSystem profile, which says that the element CodeSystem.experimental is mandatory, but it is not present
Published code systems SHOULD conform to the ShareableCodeSystem profile, which says that the element CodeSystem.caseSensitive is mandatory, but it is not present
CodeSystems SHOULD have a stated value for the caseSensitive element so that users know the status and meaning of the code system clearly
Published code systems SHOULD conform to the ShareableCodeSystem profile, which says that the element CodeSystem.experimental is mandatory, but it is not present
Published code systems SHOULD conform to the ShareableCodeSystem profile, which says that the element CodeSystem.caseSensitive is mandatory, but it is not present
Best Practice Recommendation: In general, all observations should have a performer
Wrong Display Name 'Histology of primary' for https://dhis2.org/fhir/cancer-registry/CodeSystem/CRBasisDiagnosisCS#7. Valid display is 'Histology of primary (7)' (for the language(s) 'en-US')

None of the codings provided are in the value set 'CR - Basis diagnosis value set' (https://dhis2.org/fhir/cancer-registry/ValueSet/CRBasisDiagnosisVS|0.1.0), and a coding from this value set is required) (codes = https://dhis2.org/fhir/cancer-registry/CodeSystem/CRBasisDiagnosisCS#7)
Best Practice Recommendation: In general, all observations should have a performer
Wrong Display Name 'Malignant, primary site' for https://dhis2.org/fhir/cancer-registry/CodeSystem/CRBehaviourCS#3. Valid display is 'Malignant (3)' (for the language(s) 'en-US')

None of the codings provided are in the value set 'CR - Behaviour value set' (https://dhis2.org/fhir/cancer-registry/ValueSet/CRBehaviourVS|0.1.0), and a coding from this value set is required) (codes = https://dhis2.org/fhir/cancer-registry/CodeSystem/CRBehaviourCS#3)
Best Practice Recommendation: In general, all observations should have a performer
Wrong Display Name 'Grade II' for https://dhis2.org/fhir/cancer-registry/CodeSystem/CRGradeCS#2. Valid display is 'Grade II (2)' (for the language(s) 'en-US')

None of the codings provided are in the value set 'CR - Grade value set' (https://dhis2.org/fhir/cancer-registry/ValueSet/CRGradeVS|0.1.0), and a coding from this value set is required) (codes = https://dhis2.org/fhir/cancer-registry/CodeSystem/CRGradeCS#2)
Best Practice Recommendation: In general, all observations should have a performer
Best Practice Recommendation: In general, all observations should have a performer
Best Practice Recommendation: In general, all observations should have a performer

There are multiple different potential matches for the url 'http://terminology.hl7.org/ValueSet/v2-0092'. It might be a good idea to fix to the correct version to reduce the likelihood of a wrong version being selected by an implementation/implementer, or use the [IG Parameter `pin-canonicals`](https://hl7.org/fhir/tools/CodeSystem-ig-parameters.html). Using version '3.0.0', found versions: 2.0.0 (from hl7.terminology#5.5.0), 3.0.0 (from hl7.terminology.r4#7.1.0). Suggested fix: change the canonical reference from 'http://terminology.hl7.org/ValueSet/v2-0092' to 'http://terminology.hl7.org/ValueSet/v2-0092|3.0.0'
The repeating element has a pattern. The pattern will apply to all the repeats (this has not been clear to all users)
The discriminator type 'pattern' is deprecated in R5+. For future compatibility, you could consider using type=value with a pattern[x] instead (if this is not an inherited slicing)
